protocol.name: wlr_screencopy_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright © 2018 Drew DeVault
Permission to use, copy, modify, distribute, and sell this software and its documentation for any purpose is hereby granted without fee, provided that the above copyright notice appear in all copies and that both that copyright notice and this permission notice appear in supporting documentation, and that the name of the copyright holders not be used in advertising or publicity pertaining to distribution of the software without specific, written prior permission. protocol.description:
This protocol allows clients to ask the compositor to copy part of the screen content to a client-provided buffer.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is experimental and backward incompatible changes may be made. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes are done by bumping the version number in the protocol and interface names and resetting the interface version. Once the protocol is to be declared stable, the 'z' prefix and the version number in the protocol and interface names are removed and the interface version number is reset.

interface.name: zwlr_screencopy_manager_v1

description.summary: manager to inform clients and begin capturing

interface.description: This object is a manager which offers requests to start capturing from a source.

request.name: capture_output
request.description:
Capture the next frame of an entire output.
When the compositor considers the new frame ready, the zwlr_screencopy_frame_v1.buffer_done event is sent.

request.name: capture_output_region
request.description:
Capture the next frame of a region of an output.
The region is given in output-local coordinates.
When the compositor considers the new frame ready, the zwlr_screencopy_frame_v1.buffer_done event is sent.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the manager. This request has no side-effect.

entry.name: invalid_buffer
entry.summary: the provided buffer has an invalid format

interface.name: zwlr_screencopy_frame_v1
interface.description:
This object represents a single frame.
When created, a "buffer_done" event will be sent. The client should then create a buffer, and send the "copy" request.
After the "ready" event is sent, the client should destroy the frame.

event.name: buffer
event.description: Provides information about the buffer parameters for the frame.

event.name: ready

description.summary: indicates frame is available for reading

event.description: Called as soon as the frame is copied, indicating the buffer is ready to be read by the client.

arg.name: tv_sec_hi
arg.summary: high 32 bits of the seconds part of the timestamp

arg.name: tv_sec_lo
arg.summary: low 32 bits of the seconds part of the timestamp

event.name: failed
event.description: This event indicates that the attempted copy has failed.

event.name: damage
event.description:
This event is sent right before the ready event when the compositor has not sent a buffer event since the last copy request.
The arguments describe the damaged region of the buffer, in buffer-local coordinates.

event.name: linux_dmabuf
event.description: Provides information about the linux_dmabuf buffer parameters for the frame.

event.name: buffer_done

description.summary: all buffer attributes have been sent

event.description: This event is sent once after all buffer attributes have been sent. The client should proceed to create a buffer and send the copy request.

request.name: copy
request.description:
Copy the frame to the supplied buffer. The buffer must have the correct size and format as provided by the buffer event.
If the frame is successfully copied, a "ready" event is sent. Otherwise, a "failed" event is sent.

request.name: copy_with_damage
request.description:
Same as copy, except it also provides information about the changed region.
After this request is sent, the compositor will send a "damage" event before the "ready" event if it knows the damaged region.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the frame. This request can be sent at any time by the client.
